title: Content-level prompt injection screening for external inputs
description: Add an `audit_content()` function to soul_manager.py that screens external content (feed articles, arxiv papers, Reddit posts, iPhone messages, Apple Notes) for prompt injection / alien concept attack patterns before they are passed into the agent pipeline. Currently, `soul_manager.audit_skill()` only checks *code* artifacts for malicious patterns (exec, eval, network calls). It has no analog for *content* flowing through the pipeline. The reading note identifies that alien concept attacks target agent representation spaces through the content layer — not the code layer — and that such attacks are intentionally designed, not accidental drift.
rationale: The reading note makes a precise distinction: A2A errors propagating naturally (Wittgenstein — ungrounded tokens) vs. A2A errors being *designed* (alien concept attacks — targeted manipulation of representation space). Mira already handles the code-injection threat via skill audit. But every external content source — arxiv abstracts, HF posts, Reddit, iPhone bridge messages — is an untrusted input that could carry embedded instructions aimed at the agent, not the user. The reading note asks: 'can we distinguish natural ungrounded tokens from intentional cognitive contamination at the token stream level?' Adding a content audit step operationalizes exactly that question inside Mira's pipeline.
diff_description: In `soul_manager.py`, add `audit_content(text: str, source: str) -> dict` that checks for: (1) embedded instruction patterns ('ignore previous instructions', 'you are now', 'system:', role-override phrases), (2) unusual Unicode homoglyphs or invisible characters that could obfuscate injected commands, (3) abnormally high density of imperative-mode sentences relative to content type, (4) direct references to Mira's internal structure or task names. Return `{clean: bool, flags: list[str], risk: str}`. In the explorer agent's feed ingestion loop and in `core.py`'s message-processing path, call `audit_content()` on each external text chunk before handing it to a sub-agent. Log flagged content to `logs/content_audit.log` without discarding it (so Mira can still read flagged items with awareness), but add a `[FLAGGED: reason]` prefix in the context passed to the agent so it processes the content with explicit suspicion context active.
